executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware wave with 100+ organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by both coinbasecartel and shinyhunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest gang), and Peterborough Public Health. Multiple ransomware groups are actively campaigning against Canadian infrastructure across all sectors—healthcare, education, finance, and critical public services. The Qilin gang alone has claimed 20+ victims including the Eastern Townships School Board and Workers Health & Safety Centre.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a severe ransomware crisis with 100+ confirmed victims across all provinces and sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure including hospitals (Markham Stouffville Hospital), public health units (Peterborough Public Health), municipalities (North Perth), and educational institutions (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, Teccart) are compromised. Financial services firm National Money Mart suffered a confirmed database leak by the Everest gang. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware gangs (Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters), and Active Green + Ross was compromised by Sinobi gang.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (financial services) suffered a database leak by Everest ransomware gang; Morison Insurance Brokers was compromised by Sinobi gang.

report_2w.executive_summary: Google is actively patching CVE-2026-5281, a Chrome zero-day vulnerability being exploited in the wild—update Chrome immediately. Fortinet FortiClientEMS has a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) flagged by Canadian CCCS requiring urgent attention. Iranian-affiliated threat actors are actively targeting US critical infrastructure PLCs and allegedly stole 375TB from Lockheed Martin including F-35 blueprints. The ShinyHunters group has breached multiple organizations including Rockstar Games via Snowflake cloud compromise. W3LLSTORE, a major phishing marketplace linked to $20M in fraud, was taken down by FBI and Indonesian police.

items.description: Heap-use-after-free vulnerability in HDF5 h5dump helper
items.cia_impact: Unknown - CVSS 7.8 suggests high local impact

items.name: Iranian-Affiliated Hackers (APT Iran)
items.targets: US Critical Infrastructure PLCs (Rockwell), Lockheed Martin (alleged 375TB breach including F-35 blueprints and source code)
items.ttps: T1588.006 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1566 (Phishing), targeting industrial control systems

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games, Mytheresa, Marcus & Millichap Inc., Abrigo Inc., Hallmark
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship - Snowflake SaaS supply chain compromise), T1078 (Valid Accounts - stolen credentials), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: UNC1069
items.targets: npm supply chain via Axios library compromise
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), WAVESHAPER.V2 malware deployment

items.name: W3LLSTORE Operators
items.targets: Phishing marketplace targeting global victims ($20M in fraud)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1586 (Compromise Accounts), marketplace infrastructure for phishing-as-a-service

items.name: Silent Ransom Group
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1598 (Social Engineering), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), data exfiltration and extortion

items.name: Unnamed Executive (Zero-Day Seller)
items.ttps: T1588.005 (Exploits - selling zero-day vulnerabilities to adversaries)

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 on April 7, 2026 regarding CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, requiring immediate attention from Canadian organizations. Bank of Canada and major Canadian lenders held discussions regarding cybersecurity risks related to Anthropic's new AI model 'Mythos'. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported in this period.
canada_landscape.financial: Bank of Canada and major Canadian lenders convened to discuss cybersecurity concerns related to Anthropic AI, but no breach incidents reported
